title: Market Brief — Wednesday, April 8, 2026
summary: Compute is in severe shortage across legacy silicon: H100 at 10% availability (66/294), H200 at 10% (113/400), while B200 sits at 23% (64/64) with full inventory visible but thinly traded. B200 spot surged 19.1% day-over-day to $4.69/hr with 96.2% annualized volatility, signaling speculative hoarding as buyers front-run Blackwell deployment cycles.
paragraphs: Compute is in severe shortage across legacy silicon: H100 at 10% availability (66/294), H200 at 10% (113/400), while B200 sits at 23% (64/64) with full inventory visible but thinly traded. B200 spot surged 19.1% day-over-day to $4.69/hr with 96.2% annualized volatility, signaling speculative hoarding as buyers front-run Blackwell deployment cycles.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), H100 inference runs $6.83/M tokens and H200 runs $6.18/M on Llama 70B. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is selling at a massive loss against any self-hosted benchmark, burning cash to buy market share. OpenAI's medium-tier models price near or below TCO. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Strong, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

B200 is overvalued at $4.69/hr spot given 77.4% utilization trending down 8.3pp, a classic supply-absorption lag priced as scarcity. Best risk-adjusted trade: lock H200 capacity at $2.58/hr while utilization climbs from 71% to 90% in 30 days, capturing margin expansion before spot reprices.
bullets: 10% of H100 and H200 GPUs are available, qualifying both as severe shortage markets.
23% B200 availability masks thin liquidity with only 64 total tracked GPUs.
$4.69 B200 median spot represents a 19.1% single-day price spike.
21.41 LLMflation index means output tokens cost 79% less than at GPT-4 launch.
$0.38 per million output tokens from DeepSeek V3.2 is roughly $6 below TCO-adjusted H100 production cost.
89.5% H200 utilization surged 18.2 percentage points in 30 days, the fastest tightening across all GPU classes.
